description: Rewire App.jsx to use the new persistenceService for all read/write operations. Replace the monolithic autosave with 3 independent debounced useEffects. Update initialization, project CRUD, import/export, and project switching to use the per-workspace persistence API. Maintain backward compatibility for old export formats.
steps: 1. UPDATE IMPORTS in src/App.jsx: Add import of key functions from './persistenceService' (initializePersistence, saveProjectMeta, saveWorkspace, saveTasks, saveMeta, loadWorkspace, loadTasks, generateId, createDebouncedSaver, detectMigrationNeeded). Keep existing firebaseService imports temporarily for backward compat during transition (they can be removed once all paths use the new service).

2. REPLACE INITIALIZATION (lines ~656-900): Replace the existing init useEffect body with a call to initializePersistence(). Map the returned data structure to the existing React state variables (projects/setProjects can become a metadata map, workspaces/setWorkspaces loaded from per-workspace data, tasks from per-project tasks, etc.). Keep the password hashing migration logic. Keep the workspaceValidator calls. Keep the reminders loading. The initialization should: detect migration, run it if needed, then load via new API. Maintain the existing Firestore-first-then-localStorage fallback order. Keep setSyncStatus updates.

3. REPLACE AUTOSAVE (lines ~1060-1130): Remove the single debounced useEffect that watches [workspaces, activeTab, nextId, reminders, tasks, taskGroups]. Replace with 3 independent useEffects using createDebouncedSaver: (a) Workspace autosave (300ms): watches [workspaces, activeTab] - saves only the changed workspace document(s) to localStorage (cm-ws-{projectId}-{workspaceId}) and Firestore (saveWorkspaceToFirestore). Track which workspace changed by comparing with a ref. (b) Task autosave (500ms): watches [tasks, taskGroups] - saves cm-tasks-{projectId} and saveTasksToFirestore. (c) Metadata autosave (200ms): watches [nextId, reminders] - saves cm-proj-{projectId} and saveProjectToFirestore. All three update the lastModified timestamp on the project metadata. All three set syncStatus. Keep the projectsRef pattern for accessing latest state in timeouts.

4. UPDATE PROJECT CREATION (createProject function around line 2146): Use generateId() for new project IDs and workspace IDs instead of Date.now(). Write new project to cm-proj-{id}, write initial workspace to cm-ws-{id}-{wsId}, write empty tasks to cm-tasks-{id}, update cm-meta. Also fire-and-forget write to Firestore. Keep the existing projects state array update for in-memory consistency.

5. UPDATE PROJECT DELETION (deleteProject function around line 2487): Remove cm-proj-{id}, all cm-ws-{id}-* keys, cm-tasks-{id} from localStorage. Also delete from Firestore (project doc and subcollections). Update cm-meta if deleting default/active project.

6. UPDATE PROJECT SWITCHING (switchProject around line 2192, cycleToProject around line 2256): Save current project state to new per-workspace keys before switching. Load target project's workspaces from cm-ws-{targetId}-{wsId} keys (or from Firestore if localStorage miss). Update cm-meta with new activeProjectId. Remove the full blob writes (localStorage.setItem('nexus-app-state', JSON.stringify(updated))).

7. UPDATE WORKSPACE ADD/DUPLICATE (addWorkspace around line 1982, duplicateWorkspace around line 2019): Use generateId() for new workspace IDs. After creating workspace, immediately write to cm-ws-{projectId}-{newWsId} and update project metadata's workspaceIds array.

8. UPDATE EXPORT (exportAllData around line 2437): Assemble full backup from per-workspace storage. For each project, gather metadata from cm-proj-{id}, workspaces from cm-ws-{id}-{wsId} keys, tasks from cm-tasks-{id}. Construct the same export format (type: 'thoughtflow-backup', version: 2, with projects array containing assembled project objects). Keep backward-compatible structure.

9. UPDATE IMPORT (importAllData around line 2675): After parsing and validating the imported data, decompose into per-workspace keys using the new API. Handle both v1 (old format with inline workspaces) and v2 (new format). Write cm-proj-{id}, cm-ws-{id}-{wsId}, cm-tasks-{id} for each imported project. Update cm-meta. Fire-and-forget sync to Firestore.
10. UPDATE PASSWORD CHANGE (changeProjectPassword around line ~2540): Save password to cm-proj-{projectId} and sync to Firestore project doc.

11. REMOVE LEGACY BLOB WRITES: Remove all remaining localStorage.setItem('nexus-app-state', ...) calls that occur in project creation, deletion, switching, import, and password change. The only remaining reference to 'nexus-app-state' should be in the migration detection code and the initialization fallback. Remove localStorage.setItem('nexus-active-project', ...) and localStorage.setItem('nexus-default-project', ...) calls - these are replaced by cm-meta.

12. KEEP BACKWARD COMPAT: Do NOT remove the ability to read 'nexus-app-state' during initialization (it is the migration source and final fallback). Keep the 'nexus-full-backup' type recognition in importAllData. Keep workspaceValidator.js calls intact (it receives workspace arrays which remain the same shape).
13. Run `npm run build` and fix any compilation errors.
acceptance_criteria: App.jsx imports and uses persistenceService functions for all persistence operations
Three independent debounced autosave useEffects exist (workspace 300ms, tasks 500ms, metadata 200ms)
New projects and workspaces use UUID-based IDs via generateId()
Project switching saves/loads via per-workspace keys instead of full blob
Import/export still works with both v1 and v2 formats
No remaining writes to 'nexus-app-state' localStorage key (reads allowed for migration/fallback)
verification: Run `npm run build` in /projects/sandbox/Clear-mind and confirm it exits 0 with no errors
findings: Build succeeds cleanly. The old firebaseService imports (loadProjects, saveProjects, loadActiveProject, loadDefaultProject, saveActiveProject, saveDefaultProject) are kept for the initialization Firestore-first loading path as backward compat during transition. The chunk size warning is pre-existing and acceptable. Export format version bumped to 2 but import still handles both v1 and v2 (and nexus-full-backup type). The manual sync retry button in the UI was also updated to use the new persistence service.
